{{Quote|I suppose I should ask after my niece. She's done well for herself, though I admit she and I have lost touch since you spirited her away from us.|Arkoh Adasca, to Lucien Draay|<ref name="KOTOR 19" />}}
'''Aurora Adasca''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the niece of [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Arkoh Adasca]], head of the [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania|Adasca Corporation]]. She became a [[Jedi scout|scout]] for the [[Revanchist]] movement on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Serroco]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] but was left unaccounted for after [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] [[Battle of Serroco|attacked]] the planet in [[3963 BBY]].

==Biography==
The niece of [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Arkoh Adasca]], the head of [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania]], Aurora Adasca was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] who lived in the period of [[Time/Legends|time]] that followed the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. As she was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]], Aurora was taken by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] for [[Jedi training/Legends|training]],<ref name="CSWE" /> an action her uncle likened to an abduction. Arkoh Adasca never contacted her after she was taken, nor did [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Lucien Draay]]—a family friend of the [[House of Adasca|Adascas]]—ever see her again, either.<ref name="KOTOR 19" /> Aurora eventually became a [[Jedi scout]] and traveled to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Serroco]] to assist the [[Revanchist]] movement, which aimed to involve the whole Order in the ongoing [[Mandalorian Wars]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]].<ref name="DHCb" /> After Serroco was [[Battle of Serroco|razed]] by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] in [[3963 BBY]], Aurora was left unaccounted for. As she was the sole remaining heir to the House of Adasca after her uncle was [[First Battle of Omonoth|killed]], her disappearance enabled investors of the [[Draay Trust]] to bid for the takeover of the Adasca Corporation.<ref name="TAL" />

==Behind the scenes==
Aurora Adasca was first mentioned in the [[Knights of the Old Republic 19|nineteenth issue]] of the [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']]. The issue was written by [[John Jackson Miller]]<ref name="KOTOR 19" /> and published on [[August 22]], [[2007]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54338/star_wars_knights_of_the_old_republic_2006_19|text=Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (2006) #19}}</ref> It was not until [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' that the character was named.<ref name="CSWE" />

In [[John Ostrander]]'s early outlines for ''[[Star Wars: Vector]]'', the lead protagonist was intended to be an Arkanian Jedi named Aurora. As such, John Jackson Miller planted the character of Aurora Adasca in [[Knights of the Old Republic 19|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 19]] in anticipation for the character's future appearance. However, plans for ''Vector'' ended up changing and the lead protagonist was instead a [[Human/Legends|human]] Jedi named [[Celeste Morne]].<ref name="KOTOR Countdown">{{Faraway|url=2010/02/15/kotor-countdown-4-lady-vanishes-or|text=KOTOR Countdown #4: The Lady Vanishes — or, The Mystery of Aurora}}</ref>
